title: Add Authenticated Flag to DTP_SIGNUP Table
description: Add an `Authenticated` field (0/1) to the `DTP_SIGNUP` data model. When the Salesforce OAuth callback URL is processed, the system stamps the authenticated status on the sign-up record. This allows tracking whether a user successfully authenticated through Salesforce independently of whether a user account was ultimately created.
acceptance_criteria: An Authenticated field exists on the DTP_SIGNUP table with values 0 or 1
The Authenticated field is set to 1 when the Salesforce OAuth callback completes successfully
Sign-up records accurately reflect authentication status independent of user creation
middleware_changes: Updated the Salesforce OAuth callback handler to set the Authenticated flag to 1 on the corresponding DTP_SIGNUP record upon successful authentication.
data_model_changes: Added `Authenticated` boolean field (0/1) to the `DTP_SIGNUP` table.
implementation_notes: Deployed to staging and production. Tested by creating a new user on dtpwaqar Salesforce instance, completing the OTP flow, authenticating via Salesforce, and verifying the Authenticated field was updated to 1.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed implementation on staging and production with end-to-end testing. A new user was created, went through the OTP and Salesforce auth flow, and the Authenticated field was verified as updated to 1.
